business_ideas.idea: Made Renovations turnkey bathroom remodels with 3 stock designs
Cloud kitchens (ghost restaurants optimized for delivery only)
Kava bars (psychoactive herbal drink served communally)
Religious meditation app (Halo or similar for different faiths)
Where's Waldo for Hindu gods using free religious IP

predictions.text: Elimination of choice drives conversion in high-friction categories like renovation
Delivery apps train consumers to order in, creating ghost kitchen supply opportunity
Religious content apps will grow faster than secular meditation apps

dynamics: Rapid ideation between Sam and Shaan. Sam riffs on cultural/religious angle; Shaan grounds in business mechanics (pricing, delivery, distribution). Light tone with tangents on Gen Z depression, kava effects, bugs.
frameworks: Elimination of choice via stock options (bathroom: 3 designs; travel: prepackaged bundles)
Pre-awareness marketing advantage (religious IP free globally)
Delivery-first food optimization (avoid fries, prioritize items that travel well)
learnings: Non-alcoholic beverages growing category as Gen Z drinks less alcohol and more hemp products
Small details (eliminating line-item fees, capped game length at 2 hours) signal founder care
Religious/cultural markets have undervalued advertising rates but high engagement

banger_quotes.quote: Everything is broken everything is broken we have to figure this out

numbers_dropped: Heineken CEO said fastest-growing category is 0.0 non-alcoholic beer
Travis Kalanick allocated $500M to cloud kitchen initiatives
Domino's outperformed Google stock 5x since IPO
notable: Lobby conference (VC event in Hawaii) sets context for Gen Z mental health discussion; Jamie Siminoff (Ring founder) mentioned as investing in bug solutions.
